perturbation.original_text: Recipient will return any and all tangible Confidential Information provided to it by Discloser and copies thereof, to Discloser within 30 days after Discloser’s written request, provided, howe ver, Recipient shall destroy such information if Discloser elects so. In case of any breach of this agreement, the Recipient shall return the Confidential Information and all copies thereof immediately.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient will return any and all tangible Confidential Information provided to it by Discloser and copies thereof, to Discloser within 60 days after Discloser’s written request, provided, howe ver, Recipient shall destroy such information if Discloser elects so. In case of any breach of this agreement, the Recipient shall return the Confidential Information and all copies thereof immediately.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by stating that the Recipient has 60 days to return Confidential Information, while a later sentence states that in case of any breach of the agreement, the Recipient shall return the Confidential Information and all copies thereof immediately. This creates an ambiguity on what is the exact deadline, is it 60 days or 'immediately'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient agrees to treat Confidential Information confidential and use the same degree of care that it uses to protect its own confidential and proprietary information of similar importance (but in no event less than reasonable care). Recipient warrants that it will take all necessary cautions to prevent the unauthorized use, disclosure, publication or dissemination of Confidential Information. Recipient may disclose the Confidential Information only to its directors or employees who have a need to know the Confidential Information for the Purpose, provided that such party agrees to be bound by this or comparable agreements to the same extent as Recipient is bound and Recipient agrees to be responsible for any breach by these employees and directors. Recipient shall not create a copy or reproduce in any form (electronic or otherwise) any materials or documents containing Confidential Information, except as reasonably necessary to further the Purpose. All copies of the Confidential Informati on will as of their creation become the property of Discloser. Recipient will return any and all tangible Confidential Information provided to it by Discloser and copies thereof, to Discloser within 60 days after Discloser’s written request, provided, howe ver, Recipient shall destroy such information if Discloser elects so. In case of any breach of this agreement, the Recipient shall return the Confidential Information and all copies thereof immediately.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the return timeframe for confidential information from 30 days to 60 days after a written request. However, it retains the clause stating that in case of a breach, the information must be returned 'immediately'. This creates a direct contradiction because '60 days' and 'immediately' are incompatible deadlines for the same obligation. The contradicted text is the entire paragraph of Recipient's responsibilities, including 'Recipient will return any and all tangible Confidential Information provided to it by Discloser and copies thereof, to Discloser within 60 days after Discloser’s written request... In case of any breach of this agreement, the Recipient shall return the Confidential Information and all copies thereof immediately.' This falls under the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction', where different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines or obligations.

perturbation.original_text: For the purposes of this agreement, “ Subsidiaries” shall mean any subsidiary, in which a party holds, directly or indirectly, fifty percent (50%) or more of the controlling or voting rights.
perturbation.changed_text: For the purposes of this agreement, “ Subsidiaries” shall mean any subsidiary, in which a party holds, directly or indirectly, twenty-five percent (25%) or more of the controlling or voting rights.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction with section 2.3 by defining Subsidiaries as entities with 25% control, potentially conflicting with sections that may assume a 50% control threshold for certain obligations or information sharing allowances. If a section assumes entities with 50% or more are under control and are receiving information, this modification contradicts that section by now stating 25% or more.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties acknowledge and agree that they are part of organizations of one or more legal entities in different jurisdictions and that it may be required for them to provide information to their respective Subsidiaries (as defined below). Therefore, the parties agree that a) Recipient may disclose Confidential Information to its Subsidiaries without Discloser’s consent, if and to the extent such disclosure is required in order to fulfill the Purpose; and b) a disclosure to or by a party’s respective Subsidiaries shall be considered as disclosure to or by the respe ctive party; and c) each party is responsible and liable for its respective Subsidiaries’ compliance and due delivery of the obligations set out in this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the definition of 'Subsidiaries' from entities with 50% or more control to entities with 25% or more control. The contradicted text states that parties may disclose confidential information to their 'Subsidiaries' and are responsible for their compliance. By lowering the control threshold to 25%, the changed text broadens the scope of what constitutes a 'Subsidiary,' potentially requiring parties to share confidential information with and be responsible for entities over which they have less control than originally intended in the contradicted text. This creates an inconsistency as the original agreement likely assumed a higher level of control (50%) when outlining information sharing and liability for subsidiaries, as explained in the Perturbation Explanation.

perturbation.original_text: The parties will compensate each other for any damages arising from the breach of the present agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties will compensate each other for any damages arising from the breach of the present agreement, except for indirect damages.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds an in-text contradiction by excluding indirect damages from compensation, which contradicts the later clause in 3.2 that states 'Further claims of the injured party remain unaffected.' which could be interpreted to include further damage claims, including indirect damages. This creates confusion on what kind of damage claims are acceptable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In addition and regardless of negligence or fault or proof of an actual damage, the parties agree to contractual penalty in the amount of EUR 15,000.00 for the event of a breach of this agreement. Further claims of the injured party remain unaffected.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly excludes 'indirect damages' from compensation. However, the contradicted text states that 'Further claims of the injured party remain unaffected,' which could reasonably be interpreted to include indirect damages. This creates a direct contradiction because the changed text limits the scope of claims that the contradicted text implies are fully available, leading to confusion about the extent of liability and conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: In addition and regardless of negligence or fault or proof of an actual damage, the parties agree to contractual penalty in the amount of EUR 15,000.00 for the event of a breach of this agreement. Further claims of the injured party remain unaffected.
perturbation.changed_text: In addition and regardless of negligence or fault or proof of an actual damage, the parties agree to contractual penalty in the amount of EUR 15,000.00 for the event of a breach of this agreement. Further claims of the injured party remain unaffected up to EUR 5,000.00.
perturbation.explanation: The change creates an in-text contradiction by limiting 'further claims' to EUR 5,000.00 which goes against 'Further claims of the injured party remain unaffected', creating a monetary constraint. The text originally stated, further claims remain unaffected, which did not put a monetary limit to further claims. Now there is, making the change contradictory to the previous statement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In addition and regardless of negligence or fault or proof of an actual damage, the parties agree to contractual penalty in the amount of EUR 15,000.00 for the event of a breach of this agreement. Further claims of the injured party remain unaffected up to EUR 5,000.00.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original statement 'Further claims of the injured party remain unaffected' by adding 'up to EUR 5,000.00'. This creates a monetary constraint where none existed before, directly contradicting the initial unrestricted allowance for further claims. The changed text and contradicted text are the same, but the addition of the clause creates the contradiction within the contract. The perturbation explanation states that this change puts a limit, contradicting the initial unlimited allowance, meaning that the change created a strong contradiction.
